nodes.name: 三聲夜鷹
nodes.summary: 三聲夜鷹（Antrostomus vociferus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如三聲夜鷹。

nodes.name: 卡氏夜鹰属
nodes.summary: 卡氏夜鹰属（Antrostomus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联12种鸟类，如Chuck-will's-widow、Rufous nightjar、Cuban nightjar等12种鸟类。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: NT
nodes.summary: NT表示 IUCN 近危等级，当前关联914种鸟类，如帝企鹅、小斑几维鸟、大美洲鸵等914种鸟类。

nodes.name: 三聲夜鷹
nodes.summary: 三聲夜鷹（學名：Antrostomus vociferus），是一種分布於北美的夜鷹。棲息於開闊的林地。波多黎各夜鷹亦稱為波多黎各三聲夜鷹。
nodes.shortSummary: 三聲夜鷹（學名：Antrostomus vociferus），是一種分布於北美的夜鷹。棲息於開闊的林地。波多黎各夜鷹亦稱為波多黎各三聲夜鷹。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: thumb|left|A rarely seen eastern whip-poor-will by day in Philadelphia, [[Pennsylvania]] The eastern whip-poor-will is currently in decline, though they remain fairly common. In 2017, the eastern whip-poor-will was uplisted from least concern to near threatened on the IUCN Red List on the basis of citizen science observations demonstrating a decline in populations of the eastern whip-poor-will by over 60% between 1970 and 2014. Several reasons for the decline are proposed, such as loss of early successional forest habitat related to fire suppression and habitat destruction, predation by feral cats and dogs, and poisoning by insecticides, but the actual causes remain elusive. Pesticides and intensified agriculture have led to declines in the flying insect populations that the eastern whip-poor-will depends on

In 2017, the eastern whip-poor-will was uplisted from least concern to near threatened on the IUCN Red List on the basis of citizen science observations demonstrating a decline in populations of the eastern whip-poor-will by over 60% between 1970 and 2014. Several reasons for the decline are proposed, such as loss of early successional forest habitat related to fire suppression and habitat destruction, predation by feral cats and dogs, and poisoning by insecticides, but the actual causes remain elusive. Pesticides and intensified agriculture have led to declines in the flying insect populations that the eastern whip-poor-will depends on
